name: Bordeaux Blend (Rive Gauche)
desc: Cabernet-dominant. French wines are expertly blended for balance; for example, the Merlot in this Bordeaux blend softens the firm Cabernet tannins perfectly for a Steak Frites.

name: Bordeaux Blend (Rive Droite)
desc: Merlot-dominant. The blending process ensures harmony—the lush Merlot rounds out the mouthfeel while a touch of Cabernet adds structural backbone, bringing perfect balance to rich bistro fare.

name: Burgundy (Pinot Noir)
desc: Light-bodied red with high acidity, showcasing terroir through red berry, earth, and spice. Elegant enough for poultry like Coq au Vin.

name: Côtes du Rhône / GSM Blend
desc: A masterclass in blending for balance: the Syrah adds intense dark fruit and peppery spice, while Grenache smooths the tannins into a seamless, harmonious wine.

name: White Burgundy (Chardonnay)
desc: From the crisp, mineral-driven Chablis to rich, buttery Meursault, expressing pure elegance and balanced acidity.

name: Sancerre / Pouilly-Fumé
desc: A refined Sauvignon Blanc expression. Zesty, herbaceous, and intensely mineral-driven to flawlessly contrast delicate seafood.

name: Vouvray (Chenin Blanc)
desc: Highly versatile white, showing a brilliant balance of bright acidity with slightly honeyed notes of yellow apple.

name: Châteauneuf-du-Pape / GSM Blend
desc: A Grenache-dominant blend where multiple varietals are layered to soften the structure, achieving a lush, perfectly blended balance for rustic stews.
